entities.name: IT Knowledge Base
observations: Main knowledge base for Schrödinger's IT resources and documentation
Serves as a centralized knowledge directory for applications, operating systems, and IT problem-solving
Has a search function and popular labels for navigation
Space key is ITLC

entities.name: High Performance Computing
observations: Major section of the IT Knowledge Base focused on HPC resources
Contains information about Schrödinger's HPC clusters: bolt and pdxgpu
Includes user guides, FAQs, and documentation on schedulers

entities.name: HPC User Guide
observations: Comprehensive guide for using Schrödinger's HPC clusters
Includes sections on quick tips, cluster architecture, getting started, job server, software builds, project priorities, queues, and storage
Recommends users subscribe to the cluster Google group for announcements and alerts

entities.name: Information Security
observations: Section focused on information security topics
Marked with warning symbols (☢️🚧) suggesting importance or work in progress

entities.name: Operations
observations: Major section of the IT Knowledge Base covering operational aspects of IT
Contains multiple subsections for different operational areas
Includes search functionality for finding content within the section

entities.name: Systems & Services
observations: Subsection of Operations covering IT systems and services
Contains information about cloud infrastructure, storage, and other IT services
Includes documentation on Jira and other systems

entities.name: Office Locations & Services
observations: Subsection of Operations covering office locations and services
Organized by geographic regions: Americas, Europe, and Asia-Pacific
Includes information about office resources, building access, and location-specific services

entities.name: Hardware & Equipment
observations: Subsection of Operations covering hardware and equipment
Includes information about Apple devices, Ubuntu workstations, Dell devices, and peripherals
Contains hardware policies and documentation

entities.name: Software & Applications
observations: Subsection of Operations covering software and applications
Includes information about browsers, network tools, development tools, security applications, and productivity apps
Contains an approved software list

entities.name: Policies, Documentation & Support
observations: Subsection of Operations covering policies, documentation, and support
Includes information for new employees, AV support, monitoring, and documentation standards
Contains guidance on submitting and viewing tickets with JSM

entities.name: Templates
observations: Section containing templates for creating new content in the knowledge base
Includes templates for how-to guides and troubleshooting articles
Provides standardized formats for consistent documentation

entities.name: Schedulers
observations: Subsection of High Performance Computing covering job schedulers
Includes documentation on Grid Engine and Slurm schedulers
Contains frequently used commands for both scheduler types

entities.name: Cloud & Network Infrastructure
observations: Subsection of Systems & Services covering cloud and network infrastructure
Includes information about AWS, GCP, and GitHub Enterprise
Contains documentation on cloud services and network resources

entities.name: Storage, Archives & Backups
observations: Subsection of Systems & Services covering storage, archives, and backups
Includes information about Box, network attached storage, archive servers, and FTP
Contains documentation on storage management and migration

entities.name: Portland Office
observations: Schrödinger office located in Portland, Oregon
Address: 101 SW Main Street, Suite 1300, Portland, OR 97204
Has resources including printers and wireless access
Wireless network name is 'schrodinger' with WPA2/AES encryption

entities.name: Okta
observations: Identity provider used at Schrödinger for authentication
Supports multi-factor authentication including fingerprint readers and security keys
Users are encouraged to set up multiple authentication factors
Accessible at schrodinger.okta.com

entities.name: Slurm
observations: Job scheduler used on Schrödinger's HPC clusters
Provides commands for submitting, monitoring, and managing jobs
Supports batch jobs and interactive sessions
Uses partitions (queues) to organize compute resources

entities.name: Grid Engine
observations: Alternative job scheduler used on some Schrödinger HPC clusters
Documentation includes frequently used commands

entities.name: bolt
observations: One of Schrödinger's HPC clusters
Mentioned in the HPC User Guide

entities.name: pdxgpu
observations: One of Schrödinger's HPC clusters
GPU-focused cluster
Mentioned in the HPC User Guide

entities.name: Software Governance
observations: Process for approving new software at Schrödinger
Requires submission of a software request form
Documentation is still under development (marked with TODO)

entities.name: Browsers
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes web browsers used at Schrödinger

entities.name: Network & Remote Access
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes tools for network access and remote connectivity

entities.name: Development Tools
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes tools for software development

entities.name: Security & Authentication
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes tools for security and authentication
Contains guides for Okta, passwordless SSH, and other security features

entities.name: Scientific Computing
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes tools for scientific computing and research

entities.name: Productivity Apps
observations: Category of software in the Software & Applications section
Includes productivity applications used at Schrödinger

entities.name: Fingerprint Authentication
observations: Method for authenticating with Okta using built-in fingerprint readers
Supported on Chrome on macOS and Windows and Firefox on Windows
Requires enrollment of fingerprint with Touch ID / Windows Hello
Recommended as an additional authentication factor

entities.name: Passwordless SSH
observations: Method for connecting to systems without entering a password
Documentation available for setup on Windows, Linux, and MacOS
Used for secure access to systems including HPC clusters

entities.name: JAMF
observations: Device management tool specifically designed for Apple products
Helps IT team track Mac computers and ensure they run smoothly and securely
Provides inventory tracking, remote assistance, and proactive management
Includes Self Service application for easy installation of company-approved software

entities.name: JAMF Connect
observations: Simplifies macOS authentication by linking the login process with Okta
Ensures password consistency between Okta and local macOS accounts
Enhances security and streamlines access management
Reduces the need for separate Mac credentials

entities.name: Self Service
observations: Application included with JAMF device management
Provides a curated list of company-approved applications for easy installation
Allows users to run IT-approved maintenance scripts and system updates
Enables on-demand installation of security patches and configuration profiles
Offers access to company resources such as VPN configurations, printer setups, and network settings

entities.name: MacOS Upgrade Process
observations: Process for upgrading MacOS versions on Schrödinger devices
Includes preparation steps, installation instructions, and post-installation tasks
Warns about compatibility issues with new MacOS versions
Recommends waiting for official IT support before upgrading to new major versions

entities.name: Apple Devices
observations: Category of hardware devices used at Schrödinger
Includes standard Apple devices offered as company equipment
Managed through JAMF for security and consistency
Includes MacBooks and iPads

entities.name: Network Drive Mounting
observations: Process for connecting to network drives on Mac devices
Includes instructions for CIFS connections to servers like pdx-stor and nyc-stor
Provides methods for reconnecting to shares
Explains the significance of the /Volumes path in macOS
